title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 August 2024.

items.title: **Games Done Quick**'s week-long **Flame Fatales** charity speedrunning event returns this Sunday
bullets: Raising money for the Malala Fund
Starts Sunday, 16th August at 6:15pm BST, with a Horizon Forbidden West Burning Shores 100% speedrun by RE_doc19
Event will last for 7 days and ends on Sunday 25th at 4:06am BST with a Super Mario Odyssey Talkatoo% speedrun by CheeseJay

items.title: Hugely-promising indie **The Plucky Squire** launches next month, day one on PlayStation Plus
bullets: An innovative 2D and 3D platformer based in and around the pages of a picture book, The Plucky Squire is the first project from All Possible Futures, the indie development studio set up by former Pokémon artist James Turner.
PC, PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch and Xbox Series S/X on 17th September
It'll also be a day one launch via PlayStation Plus, for anyone signed up to Sony's subscription service.

items.title: **Evercade** announces its next cartridge, **Legacy of Kain Collection**
bullets: Blood Omen: Legacy of Kain, and Legacy of Kain: Soul Reaver
The cartridge can be played on all Evercade and Super Pocket devices, including the upcoming Evercade Alpha tabletop arcade machines.
Unrelated to the rumours of a Legacy of Kain remastered collection, apparently in development for current gen consoles.

items.title: **SteamOS** support coming to ROG Ally and other handhelds
bullets: Dual-boot support for SteamOS and Windows is also being prepped for Steam Deck OLED
No planned release date for either features yet.

items.title: Epic Games Store is available now on mobile, including Fall Guys and Fortnite
bullets: iOS (EU only) Install Instructions (open on device): https://store.epicgames.com/en-US/download/ios
Android Install Instructions (open on device): https://store.epicgames.com/en-US/download/android

items.title: **Star Wars Eclipse**’s lead writer has left **Quantic Dream** to start a new studio
bullets: Adam Williams was also the lead writer on Detriot: Become Human

items.title: **Scottish Games Week** skips 2024, promises late 2025 return
bullets: The industry's current issues and a lack of government support have put the annual Scottish event on ice, but next year's comeback is already underway

items.title: **Reforged Studios** acquires **Ground Shatter** and **Extra Mile Studios**
bullets: Ground Shatter is led by founder and director James Parker, and is the developer behind roguelike deck-builder Fights in Tight Spaces.
Extra Mile Studios is led by Bobby Farmer, and is known for providing co-developer services on Revolution Software's Broken Swords series.

items.title: New **DDM **(Digital Development Management) report says the games industry is "**showing signs of a recovery**"
bullets: DDM's Games Investment Review reports "sizeable uptick" in investments, recovering ground lost in 2023
DDM suggests the half-yearly uptick suggests in just the first half of 2024, investments totalled $8.1bn – almost doubling the $4.5bn investment volume seen for the whole of 2023.

However, though Q2 was "great for games investments," the report authors say M&As reached $845m across 40 transactions, which is "a drastic decline QoQ" (-59% in value and -5% in volume over Q1’s $2.1bn across 43 transactions).

items.title: **Embracer Q1 ** results
bullets: Net Sales: SEK 7.9 billion ($754 million, down 24% year-on-year)
Net Loss: SEK 2.2 billion ($210 million, compared to loss of SEK 2.3 billion / $219.4 million for the same period last year)
Net Debt: SEK 14.3 billion ($1.37 billion, compared to SEK 16.8 billion / $1.6 billion as of June 30, 2023)
Tabletop games still generating the bulk of Embracer's revenue, net debt decreased to $1.37 billion
